exercises.solution: Round to the nearest ten: $56 \rightarrow 60$, $43 \rightarrow 40$, $28 \rightarrow 30$. Then: $60 + 40 + 30 = 130$. The estimate is **130**.
hints: Round each number to the nearest ten

exercises.solution: Round: $19 \rightarrow 20$ and $52 \rightarrow 50$. Then: $20 \times 50 = 1,000$. The estimate is **1,000**.
hints: Round each number to make the multiplication easier

exercises.prompt: Check if this answer is reasonable by estimating: $198 + 203 = 401$
exercises.solution: Estimate: $198 \rightarrow 200$ and $203 \rightarrow 200$. So $200 + 200 = 400$. The calculated answer (401) is very close to the estimate (400), so it's reasonable.
hints: Perform the operation with rounded numbers
